title: Executive Order 9577—Terminating the War Food Administration and Transferring Its Functions to the Secretary of Agriculture

flags.justification: A standard wartime-agency consolidation, transferring functions to an existing permanent department; the Secretary's delegation discretion is typical for agency-consolidation orders.
A clear, specific transfer with a defined effective date.
An agency-termination and functional-transfer action, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A termination action, not a temporary-authority grant requiring review.
Sec. 5 explicitly preserves existing orders and directives until modified or revoked by the Secretary, a protective continuity provision.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
This rolls back wartime emergency-agency structure into normal peacetime department administration — a demobilization action, consistent with EO 9672's earlier NWLB-to-NWSB pattern.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. Part of the broader WWII-to-peacetime demobilization pattern already logged in this project.
